content.problemStatement:
For any zero-indexed sequence SSS of length NNN, define a function G(S)G(S)G(S) as follows:

G(S)=∏i=0N−1(Si+i)G(S) = \prod_{i=0}^{N-1} (S_i + i)G(S)=i=0∏N−1​(Si​+i)If N=0N = 0N=0, then G(S)=1G(S) = 1G(S)=1, i.e. the empty sequence has a value of 111.

You have an array BBB, which is initially empty.

You are also given an array AAA of length NNN, as well as a parameter KKK.

For each iii from 111 to NNN in order, you must insert AiA_iAi​ at the **front** of array BBB.

So, after the iii-th insertion, BBB will look like [Ai,Ai−1,Ai−2,…,A1][A_i, A_{i-1}, A_{i-2}, \ldots, A_1][Ai​,Ai−1​,Ai−2​,…,A1​].

After each insertion, consider all sequences SSS of the form

S=(Bi1,Bi2,…,Bim)S = (B_{i_1}, B_{i_2}, \dots, B_{i_m})S=(Bi1​​,Bi2​​,…,Bim​​)
such that 1≤i1<i2<⋯<im≤∣B∣1 \le i_1 < i_2 < \dots < i_m \le |B|1≤i1​<i2​<⋯<im​≤∣B∣ and 0≤m≤K0 \le m \le K0≤m≤K.

In other words, SSS is a subsequence of BBB of size at most KKK.

Your task is to compute ∑G(S)\sum G(S)∑G(S) over all such subsequences SSS.

Since the answers can be very large, output them modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of integers being inserted, and the upper bound on the length of subsequences under consideration.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots,  A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines - the iii-th line must contain the answer after iii insertions, modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

After the first insertion, B=[5]B = [5]B=[5].
Its subsequences are [],[5][], [5][],[5], so the answer is 1+5=61 + 5 = 61+5=6.
After the second insertion, B=[4,5]B = [4, 5]B=[4,5].
Its subsequences are [],[4],[5],[4,5][], [4], [5], [4, 5][],[4],[5],[4,5], so the answer is 1+4+5+4⋅(5+1)=341 + 4 + 5 + 4\cdot (5+1) = 341+4+5+4⋅(5+1)=34.
